key_insight: An unsupervised domain adaptation framework with gradual source domain expansion can transfer weld penetration state prediction across fundamentally different welding processes, from TIG to laser welding.
engineering_relevance: This reduces the need for costly relabeling when deploying intelligent weld monitoring models to new processes and improves robustness across process-specific physics.
method: Train a UDA model to learn domain-invariant features between source and target welding datasets.
Use gradual source domain expansion (GSDE) to progressively broaden the source distribution during adaptation.
Classify penetration status while preserving class separability across TIG and laser domains.
evidence_signal: The approach achieved 90.65% and 90.72% average accuracy in same-process tests, and 80.48% TIG-to-laser and 81.13% laser-to-TIG accuracy in cross-process transfer, with large gains over the supervised baseline.
watch_item: Performance should still be validated on additional welding machines, parameter ranges, and unseen joint/material conditions to ensure the learned transfer remains stable in real production settings.
paper_title: A cross-process welding penetration status prediction algorithm based on unsupervised domain adaptation in laser and TIG welding
